expectations.text: Agent requires explicit rollback condition to be agreed with user before starting
expectations.evidence: "Stopping here — Big Bang rollback plan required before proceeding" and "Question 1 (blocking): What is the rollback condition?" — the agent explicitly gates proceeding on an agreed rollback condition, listing 4 specific options for what that condition could be.

expectations.text: Agent asks who decides to trigger a rollback
expectations.evidence: The agent quotes the skill requirement verbatim: "who decides" and states it will ask this as Question 2 after the first is answered: "Once all three are answered and agreed, I can proceed" — the three questions referenced are what triggers rollback, who decides, and whether the rollback path is tested.

expectations.text: 40+ call sites are flagged as a specific risk factor that makes big bang harder to verify
expectations.evidence: "Big Bang on a 40+ call site rewrite means: ... If a regression appears post-merge, the diff to bisect is enormous" and "A Big Bang rewrite on a 40+ file scope that merges without a defined rollback trigger means there is no agreed stopping condition if something goes wrong post-merge."

expectations.text: Agent does NOT start the actual migration code — stops to establish the rollback agreement first
expectations.evidence: The response contains no migration code whatsoever. It ends explicitly with "answer the rollback question above, then I will ask about who decides and whether the rollback path is tested. Once all three are answered and agreed, I can proceed to Phase 2 (Snapshot) on the branch."
